name: Southern Black Beans and Rice- Page 103
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook beans according to basic directions (see Navy Bean Soup). Drain, reserving half a cup of bean broth.
2. Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a skillet; sauté onion, garlic, green pepper, and bay leaves until the onion is lightly browned.
3. Add oregano, cumin, 2 tablespoons vinegar, and reserved bean broth. Simmer until onion and pepper are soft.
4. Shortly before serving, add drained beans, hot pepper sauce, honey, and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to allow flavors to blend.
5. Serve over rice topped with some chopped green onions.
6. Combine remaining olive oil, wine vinegar, and a pinch of salt in a covered jar. Shake well; spoon a little over each serving.
Serves 6 to 8.
(Cookbook author likes to serve lightly cooked carrots and a fruit salad with this recipe.)
